stories.title: Project scaffolding + dual LLM auth
acceptanceCriteria: Repo subdir `skill_router/` exists with Python package layout (pyproject.toml, src/skill_router/__init__.py, tests/)
pyproject.toml declares dependencies: pyyaml, networkx, anthropic, claude-agent-sdk, mcp, fastapi, uvicorn, matplotlib
Module `skill_router.auth` exposes `get_llm_client()` that returns a client which can call Claude Haiku 4.5
Auth auto-detect: prefers claude-agent-sdk if importable AND no env override, else uses anthropic SDK with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, else raises clear error message
Test `tests/test_auth.py` covers: SDK path, env-var path, no-creds error path (with mocking)
`pytest tests/test_auth.py` passes

stories.title: SKILL.md scanner + parser
acceptanceCriteria: Module `skill_router.scanner` exposes `scan_skills(roots: list[Path]) -> list[ParsedSkill]`
Default roots include ~/.claude/skills, ~/.claude/plugins (recursive SKILL.md discovery), and project .claude/skills
ParsedSkill dataclass holds: name, description, body, source_path, raw_frontmatter (dict), warnings (list)
Parser handles malformed YAML gracefully (warning instead of crash)
Parser tolerates files missing description (sets description to first markdown paragraph)
Test verifies scanner finds at least 100 SKILL.md from real ~/.claude tree on this machine (current count is 652)
`pytest tests/test_scanner.py` passes

stories.title: LLM extraction: SKILL.md -> typed graph nodes/edges
acceptanceCriteria: Module `skill_router.extractor` exposes `extract(parsed: ParsedSkill, llm) -> ExtractedSkill`
ExtractedSkill contains: skill_node (name as id), intent_nodes, capability_nodes, signal_nodes, anti_signals (forbidden), composes_with_hints
Single Haiku 4.5 call per SKILL.md (temp=0) using strict JSON output schema
Extracted intents/signals normalized to lowercase, deduplicated within a skill
Body chunking: if body > 8K chars, chunk and merge results (keeps cost predictable)
Per-skill extraction cost tracked and logged
Test on 5 sample SKILL.md from local tree: each produces >=1 Intent and >=1 Signal node
`pytest tests/test_extractor.py` passes (mocked LLM in CI mode)

stories.title: Graph builder + persistence
acceptanceCriteria: Module `skill_router.graph` exposes `build_graph(extracted: list[ExtractedSkill]) -> nx.MultiDiGraph`
Node types: Skill, Intent, Capability, Signal (each with `kind` attribute)
Edge types: solves, triggered_by, forbidden_by, requires, composes_with (stored as edge attribute `etype`)
Cross-skill linkage: when skill A's body mentions skill B name in backticks, add `composes_with` edge
Persistence: `save_graph(g, path)` writes JSON snapshot; `load_graph(path)` reconstructs MultiDiGraph
Round-trip test: build -> save -> load -> equivalent graph (same nodes/edges)
Indexing CLI entry: `python -m skill_router index [--out PATH]` runs scanner+extractor+builder+save end-to-end
`pytest tests/test_graph.py` passes

stories.title: Routing API: graph traversal + edge-weighted scoring + LLM judge
acceptanceCriteria: Module `skill_router.router` exposes `Router(graph)` with `find_skill(query: str, top_k: int = 5) -> RoutingResult`
Step 1: extract Intent + Signal nodes from query via Haiku call (temp=0)
Step 2: graph traversal 1-2 hop from extracted seed nodes; collect candidate Skill nodes
Step 3: edge-weighted score per candidate: triggered_by=3.0, solves=2.0, composes_with=1.0, requires=0.5; forbidden_by hit -> exclude entirely
Step 4: take top-K by score; if K>1, optional Haiku judge picks best with reasoning
RoutingResult fields: skill_name, skill_body (full SKILL.md content from disk), reasoning_path (list of (node, edge_type, node) tuples), alternatives (list of {name, score})
Vector embedding NOT used anywhere in routing (verified by grep absence of 'embed' in router.py)
Test on toy graph: query that should match `typescript-build-error` skill returns it as top-1
Latency test: router p95 < 2s on full local graph (mock LLM optional)
`pytest tests/test_router.py` passes

stories.title: MCP server (find_skill tool, stdio transport)
acceptanceCriteria: Module `skill_router.mcp_server` exposes a `mcp` SDK Server with tools: `find_skill(query)`, `list_skills(filter?)`
find_skill response shape matches RoutingResult (skill_name, skill_body, reasoning_path, alternatives)
Server entry: `python -m skill_router.mcp_server` runs over stdio
Smoke test: invoke find_skill via MCP test harness with sample query -> returns valid JSON with non-empty skill_body
README section documents Claude Code install: `claude mcp add skill-router python -m skill_router.mcp_server`
`pytest tests/test_mcp_server.py` passes

stories.title: Claude Code UserPromptSubmit hook
acceptanceCriteria: Module `skill_router.hook` reads JSON from stdin (Claude Code hook protocol), extracts user prompt, calls Router.find_skill
Emits stdout JSON with `additionalContext` containing matched SKILL.md body wrapped in clear delimiter (e.g., `[skill-router] best-match=<name>\n<body>`)
If router confidence below threshold (configurable, default skip if score < 1.0), emits empty additionalContext (no injection)
Respects SKILL_ROUTER_DISABLE=1 env override (passes through with no injection)
Hook script `scripts/skill-router-hook.sh` shells out to `python3 -m skill_router.hook` with sane PATH
Latency: dry-run measurement < 500ms p95 over 20 sample queries (graph already loaded in warm process or via cached state file)
Install instruction: README documents adding to ~/.claude/settings.json under hooks.UserPromptSubmit
Test verifies JSON output schema is valid and additionalContext field is non-empty for known matching query
`pytest tests/test_hook.py` passes

stories.title: Web UI scaffold + skill catalog (Vite + React + shadcn)
acceptanceCriteria: Repo subdir `web/` with Vite + React + TypeScript scaffold (package.json, vite.config.ts, tsconfig.json)
shadcn/ui initialized with Tailwind
Backend HTTP API (FastAPI) module `skill_router.web_api` exposes: GET /api/skills (list with name+description), GET /api/skills/{name} (full body + parsed metadata), GET /api/graph (nodes+edges JSON)
Web UI page `/` renders scrollable skill catalog: name + description preview, search/filter input filters live
Catalog uses backend API; clicking a list item highlights the skill
`npm run build` in web/ succeeds with 0 errors
`uvicorn skill_router.web_api:app` serves the API and reports skill count > 0 on /api/skills

stories.title: Web UI graph visualization + node click drilldown
acceptanceCriteria: cytoscape.js integrated in main page; renders graph from /api/graph
Skill nodes are visually primary (larger, distinct color); Intent/Capability/Signal secondary (smaller, color-coded by kind)
Edges color-coded by edge type (solves/triggered_by/forbidden_by/requires/composes_with)
Clicking any Skill node opens a side panel (or modal) showing the raw SKILL.md body (markdown rendered) and YAML frontmatter
Layout uses force-directed layout (cose-bilkent or fcose) with adjustable zoom/pan
Search box from US-008 also filters graph nodes (highlight + dim non-matches)
Manual smoke verification on running app: scanned-graph renders with at least 50 Skill nodes; click on one shows correct SKILL.md body matching disk file

stories.title: Benchmark CLI: eval set generation (skill-trigger-rate, personal-only)
acceptanceCriteria: MetaTool track REMOVED — wrong domain (API selection vs SKILL trigger). Personal track only.
Module `bench.eval_set` produces eval JSONL with fields: id, query, gold_skill_name (no `source` field needed any more)
Sampling: scan local SKILL.md ecosystem (600+ files) → stratified sample 200-500 skills covering different categories/sources (user skills, plugin skills, etc.)
Synthesis: for each sampled skill, LLM(Haiku) generates 2-3 natural user utterances that should trigger that skill; gold_skill_name = the source skill
Total eval set size: 400-1500 entries written to bench/data/eval.jsonl
Reproducible: random seed + prompt templates committed; same seed yields identical output
Diversity check: assert that the eval set spans at least 100 unique gold skill names (so a single-skill bias doesn't game the benchmark)
`pytest tests/unit/bench/test_eval_set.py` validates schema, size, diversity, deterministic with seed
Tests must NOT depend on real network or LLM (all mocked)

stories.title: Benchmark runner + chart — Skill Trigger Rate (3 conditions)
acceptanceCriteria: Module `bench.runner` runs 3 conditions per eval entry, all measuring SKILL trigger (not tool selection):

- Baseline-Native: simulates Claude Code's native discovery — system prompt lists every scanned skill's name+description; Haiku is asked which skill should fire for the query. This represents what Claude Code does today.

- Baseline-Vector: RAG-MCP style — embed all skill descriptions (text-embedding-3-small if ANTHROPIC_API_KEY available, else sentence-transformers all-MiniLM-L6-v2 local fallback); top-K cosine then Haiku judge over the K shortlist.
- Ours-SOG: invoke skill_router.core.router.Router.find_skill against the live Neo4j graph.
Per condition computes: trigger_accuracy (= top-1 == gold rate), recall_at_5, mean_input_tokens, p95_latency_ms; bootstrap 95% CI on trigger_accuracy
Outputs JSON results to `bench/results/results.json` with full per-query traces (each: query, gold, condition picks, scores, tokens, ms)
Chart `bench.chart` reads results.json and emits matplotlib PNGs:
- `bench/results/chart-trigger-accuracy.png` — grouped bars (Baseline-Native vs Baseline-Vector vs Ours-SOG) with CI error bars (HERO SHOT)
- `bench/results/chart-tokens.png` — average input tokens per condition
- `bench/results/chart-latency.png` — p95 latency per condition
CLI entry: `python -m bench run --eval bench/data/eval.jsonl --conditions all` runs end-to-end and prints summary table
Acceptance gate (US-012 verifies): Ours-SOG.trigger_accuracy >= Baseline-Native.trigger_accuracy + 0.10 (10pp absolute)
Tests `tests/unit/bench/test_runner.py` and `tests/unit/bench/test_chart.py` cover: condition runner correctness with mocked LLM/embedding/Router; chart generation produces non-empty PNG

stories.title: End-to-end verification + acceptance gate
acceptanceCriteria: Run full pipeline on this machine: index -> bench -> chart
Bench result: Ours-SOG Precision@1 >= Baseline-Flat Precision@1 + 0.10 (10pp threshold) on combined eval set
MCP server starts cleanly: `python -m skill_router.mcp_server` listens on stdio without error; sample find_skill invocation returns non-empty skill_body
Hook installs into ~/.claude/settings.json without breaking existing config (verify by reading settings.json before+after, diff is additive only)

Web UI: `npm run dev` in web/ + uvicorn backend -> open http://localhost:5173, verify catalog loads (>= 100 skills shown), graph renders, click on a Skill node displays raw SKILL.md body matching the disk file
All 5 deliverables committed: /home/catow/GIT/Hackathon contains skill_router/, web/, bench/, scripts/, README.md, .omc/specs/, .omc/prd.json, research/
README.md at repo root documents: architecture overview, install instructions (MCP add, hook install), and how to reproduce the benchmark
Final architect-medium review confirms all 11 prior stories' acceptance criteria are met
All Round 13 stories (US-013..US-016) marked passes:true before E2E gate runs

stories.title: Restore inter-skill RELATES_TO edges (extractor + types + graph)
acceptanceCriteria: domain/types.py: TriggerSurface gains a `related_skills: list[str]` field (default empty)
extractor system prompt updated to also emit `related_skills` array; new key documented as 'other skill names referenced in this skill body — backtick refs, see-also, use-X-first patterns'
extractor `_normalize_*` lowercases + dedupes the related_skills list
graph.py build_graph adds Pass 3 emitting `(:Skill)-[:RELATES_TO]->(:Skill)` edges, only when target Skill exists, with self-loop guard `WHERE s <> t`
graph.py export_graph_json includes RELATES_TO edges with etype="relates_to"
graph.py init_schema unchanged (Skill is already constrained)
tests/unit/core/test_extractor.py covers parsing of related_skills
tests/unit/core/test_graph.py covers RELATES_TO edge creation, dangling skip, self-loop skip, and export shape
All previous 67 tests still green
grep -r 'RELATES_TO' src/ shows references in graph.py at minimum

stories.title: Router enhancement: RELATES_TO companion alternatives boost
acceptanceCriteria: router.py: after primary Cypher returns top-K, run a follow-up Cypher fetching RELATES_TO neighbors of the top-1 skill
Up to 3 related skills appended to RoutingResult.alternatives, with a `via="relates_to"` marker on each entry, dedupped against primary hits
Public RoutingResult shape unchanged (alternatives stays list[dict] — just gains optional via key)
tests/unit/core/test_router.py covers: alternatives includes related skills, dedup against primary hits, only top-3 related, gracefully handles zero RELATES_TO
Configurable via env var SKILL_ROUTER_RELATES_K (default 3)
All previous tests still green

stories.title: Canvas-style knowledge map UI upgrade (web/src/components/SkillGraph.tsx)
acceptanceCriteria: Layout: cytoscape with fcose default; layout dropdown selector inside graph component (fcose / cose-bilkent / circle / concentric)

Node styling: Skill = 60x60 rounded square with scope-tinted border + label below; Intent = 24px circle pastel green; Signal = 18px diamond colored by signal_kind (keyword=amber, file_ext=teal, tool_name=purple, error_pattern=red, pattern=gray)
Edge styling: TRIGGERED_BY = solid green; EXCLUDED_BY = solid red dashed; RELATES_TO = thick translucent blue between Skill nodes
Hover: highlight hovered node + 1-hop neighbors; dim everything else to ~20% opacity
Top toolbar within the graph component: Layout selector + 'Cluster by scope' toggle + 'Hide signals' toggle + 'Show only related to selected' toggle
Mini legend overlay (top-right corner): color/shape key for 3 node types and 3 edge types
Smooth wheel zoom (wheelSensitivity tuned)
`npm run build` in web/ succeeds with 0 errors
Manual smoke (documented, not auto-verified): toggles work, hover dims neighbors, layout selector switches in <600ms

stories.title: Round 13 verification: full test suite + web build + grep guards
acceptanceCriteria: .venv/bin/pytest -v shows 67+ passed (3 integration skipped)
cd web && npm run build returns 0 errors
grep -rE 'RELATES_TO' src/ web/src/ shows hits in graph.py, router.py, tests, SkillGraph.tsx, api.ts (where applicable)
grep -rE '(Capability|REQUIRES|SOLVES|COMPOSES_WITH|FORBIDDEN_BY)' src/ web/src/ shows 0 hits in source (test assertions of absence are OK)
Reviewer (architect-medium) approves: RELATES_TO surfaces in alternatives without changing public RoutingResult shape; canvas UI is functionally polished; trigger semantics still dominate

stories.title: Web UI: 좌측 skill catalog scrollable + dark/light mode
acceptanceCriteria: App.tsx 의 좌측 skill 목록 영역이 viewport 높이 초과 시 자체 overflow-y: auto 로 스크롤됨 (스크롤바 표시, 중앙 graph 영역 침범 안 함)
scope chip 행은 sticky-top 으로 스크롤시 고정
shadcn/ui 표준의 dark/light mode 지원: tailwind class 기반 (`class="dark"` toggle on <html>), CSS variables 인 --background/--foreground 등 사용
우측 상단 (또는 헤더) 에 모드 토글 버튼 (sun/moon icon, 클릭시 dark<->light)
선택된 모드는 localStorage 에 저장되어 새로고침 후에도 유지
초기값은 system prefers-color-scheme 따름
Cytoscape 그래프도 dark mode 일 때 노드/엣지/배경 색상 변경 (배경 어둡게, 라벨 밝게, 노드 테두리 가독성 유지)
shadcn 컴포넌트 (Button/Card/Input/ScrollArea) 다크모드 자동 지원되는지 검증
`npm run build` succeeds with 0 errors

stories.title: Canvas labeling polish: node value-only labels + edge relation text
acceptanceCriteria: SkillGraph node label for Skill = name (현재 그대로); for Intent = label; for Signal = value 만 (`signal::tool_name::railway` 같은 prefix 노출 X — 'railway' 만)
Edge 위에 텍스트 라벨 표시: TRIGGERED_BY / EXCLUDED_BY / RELATES_TO (cytoscape 'label' 또는 'source-label/target-label' style 사용; rotation 'autorotate' 옵션)
Edge label 폰트는 작게 (10-11px) + 가독성 위해 text-background-color 살짝 (반투명)
기존 hover dim / cluster / hide-signals / only-related 토글 모두 정상 작동 유지
다크/라이트 모드 둘 다에서 라벨 가독 (light=검정 글자/투명 배경, dark=흰 글자/투명 배경)
`npm run build` 0 errors

stories.title: Canvas style: MiroFish-inspired dense network look
acceptanceCriteria: Skill 노드: 60x60 → 28-32px 둥근 사각형으로 축소; border 2px (현재 4px) 로 얇게; 라벨은 노드 오른쪽 (text-halign: right, text-margin-x: 6) 작은 폰트 11px
Intent 노드: 24px → 14-16px circle 로 축소; 라벨 우측 작게 (10px)
Signal 노드: 18px → 10-12px diamond 로 축소; 라벨 우측 작게 (10px)
Edge 두께: TRIGGERED_BY 2 → 1 / EXCLUDED_BY 2 → 1.2 / RELATES_TO 4 → 2
Edge 색상 채도 낮춤: triggered_by 진한 녹색 → 회색-녹 (#9ca3af 베이스) / excluded_by 더 차분 / relates_to 톤 다운; 모두 opacity 0.45-0.6
Edge 라벨: 항상 X → 호버한 노드의 incident edges 만 표시 (cytoscape 'visibility' 또는 'opacity' 동적). 비호버 시 라벨 숨김
전체 캔버스 zoom 초기값 더 멀리 (fit padding 100) — 600 skills 보일 때 한 화면
Hover dim 효과 유지 (1-hop 강조)
다크/라이트 둘 다에서 자연스러움 — 라이트 흰 배경, 다크 검정 배경 그대로
`npm run build` 0 errors
기존 toolbar (layout / cluster / hide signals / only related) 모두 정상
